name: Memory Ordering & Barriers
def: Rules and fences governing the visible order of memory operations across threads/cores.
problem: Reason about what one thread sees of another’s writes when CPUs and compilers reorder operations.
mechanism: Memory models define orderings (relaxed, acquire, release, seq-cst); barriers/fences and atomic ordering tags constrain reordering to establish happens-before.
benefits: Performance via relaxed orderings where safe
limits: Among the hardest topics in programming
whenNot: High-level code shielded by locks/std structures
myths: "The CPU executes my writes in order" — cores and compilers reorder unless told otherwise
